query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Actuarial', 'Of or pertaining to actuaries', 'The author hoped the new policy would actuarial tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Actuary', 'Registrar, clerk', 'Scholars noted a striking actuary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acuity', 'Sharpness or acuteness, as of a needle, wit, etc', 'Scholars noted a striking acuity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acumen', 'The ability to make good judgments quickly', 'The essay highlights the acumen of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acute', 'Sharp', 'Her acute became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adage', 'A proverb or short saying expressing wisdom', 'The essay highlights the adage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adamant', 'Refusing to change one''s mind', 'What seemed adamant at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adapt', 'To adjust to new conditions', '“He adapted quickly to the colder climate.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Addendum', 'An addition or change', 'Scholars noted a striking addendum between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Addle', 'To make or become confused', 'The author hoped the new policy would addle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adduce', 'To cite (as proof)', 'Critics argued that the plan might adduce the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adept', 'Very skilled or proficient at something', 'The passage presents a adept portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adhere', 'To stick fast or cleave, to become joined or united', 'Critics argued that the plan might adhere the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adhesion', 'The ability of a substance to stick to an unlike substance', 'Critics argued that the plan might adhesion the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adieu', 'A farewell, a goodbye, especially a fond farewell', 'The essay highlights the adieu of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjacent', 'Close, or contiguous, neighboring, bordering on', 'The passage presents a adjacent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjourn', 'To postpone', 'The author hoped the new policy would adjourn tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjudge', 'To declare to be', 'The author hoped the new policy would adjudge tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjudicate', 'To settle a legal case or other dispute', 'The author hoped the new policy would adjudicate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjunct', 'Attached to another in a dependent or subordinate position', 'Critics argued that the plan might adjunct the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjuration', 'A grave warning', 'Scholars noted a striking adjuration between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adjutant', 'A military officer', 'The essay highlights the adjutant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Administrator', 'One who administers affairs, one who directs or manages', 'The essay highlights the administrator of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Admissible', 'Accepted or allowed, acceptable', 'Scholars noted a striking admissible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Admittance', 'The act of admitting', 'Her admittance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Admonish', 'To warn or reprimand firmly', 'Critics argued that the plan might admonish the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ado', 'To do, in doing, as, there is nothing ado', 'Over time, the community learned to ado its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adoration', 'An act of religious worship', 'Scholars noted a striking adoration between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adorn', 'To decorate', 'The author hoped the new policy would adorn tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adroit', 'Clever or skillful', 'Her adroit became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adulation', 'Excessive praise or admiration', 'The essay highlights the adulation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adulterate', 'To make poorer in quality by adding another substance', 'Over time, the community learned to adulterate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adumbrate', 'To foreshadow vaguely', 'The author hoped the new policy would adumbrate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Advent', 'Coming, arrival', 'The passage presents a advent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adventitious', 'From an external source, not innate or inherent', 'The reviewer described the tone as adventitious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adversary', 'An opponent or enemy', 'The essay highlights the adversary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adverse', 'Harmful or unfavorable', 'What seemed adverse at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Adversity', 'Difficulties or misfortune', '“Despite adversity, she remained determined to succeed.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Advert', 'To turn attention', 'The author hoped the new policy would advert tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Advisory', 'Able to give advice', 'Over time, the community learned to advisory its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Advocacy', 'Active support', 'The essay highlights the advocacy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Advocate', 'To publicly support or recommend', 'Over time, the community learned to advocate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aegi', 'The shield or breastplate of zeus or athena, protection', 'Her aegi became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aerial', 'Of, in, or from the air, atmospheric', 'Scholars noted a striking aerial between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aeronaut', 'One who glides through the air in an airship or balloon', 'The essay highlights the aeronaut of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aerostat', 'An aircraft that derives its lift from buoyancy', 'Scholars noted a striking aerostat between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aesthete', 'A person who has refined sensitivity towards art or nature', 'Her aesthete became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aesthetic', 'Concerned with beauty or artistic taste', 'What seemed aesthetic at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Affable', 'Friendly and easy to talk to', 'Critics argued that the plan might affable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Affect', 'Having an influence on or causing a change in something', 'The passage presents a affect portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
